function.name: systemd.enable
function.description: Enable a systemd unit to start on boot

user.description: Target user units instead of system units (default: false)

now.description: Also start the unit immediately (default: false)

function.name: systemd.disable
function.description: Disable a systemd unit from starting on boot

user.description: Target user units instead of system units (default: false)

now.description: Also stop the unit immediately (default: false)

function.name: systemd.status
function.description: Get the status of a systemd unit including active state, PID, and recent logs

user.description: Target user units instead of system units (default: false)

function.name: systemd.list
function.description: List systemd units with optional filtering

user.description: List user units instead of system units (default: false)

pattern.description: Glob pattern to filter unit names

function.name: systemd.journal
function.description: Query the systemd journal for log entries

since.description: Show entries since this time (e.g., '1 hour ago', '2024-01-01', 'today')

lines.description: Number of recent lines to show (default: 50)

grep.description: Filter log lines by regex pattern

function.name: systemd.timer_list
function.description: List all systemd timers with next/last trigger times

user.description: List user timers instead of system timers (default: false)

function.name: systemd.timer_create
function.description: Create a new systemd timer and associated service unit

command.description: Command to execute when timer fires

user.description: Create as user unit instead of system unit (default: false)
